output:
# BLUNT and PACT: Treating Safety-vs-Helpfulness Arbitration as a Per-Prompt Calibrated Field instead of a Global Hard Rule in RLHF Reward Aggregation

## 1. Background and Current State of the Art

Modern "product" LLMs are manufactured by a two-stage recipe that the Llama 2 report [1] both exemplifies and documents in unusual detail: an auto-regressive transformer is pretrained on a large self-supervised corpus, then ALIGNED to human preferences through supervised fine-tuning (SFT) followed by Reinforcement Learning with Human Feedback (RLHF) [2, 3, 4]. The Llama 2 introduction states the thesis of the whole alignment line plainly -- closed models "are heavily fine-tuned to align with human preferences, which greatly enhances their usability and safety," and the paper's stated contribution is to make that safe fine-tuning REPRODUCIBLE for the community ("we hope that this openness will enable the community to reproduce fine-tuned LLMs and continue to improve the safety of those models"). The central, recurring tension the report surfaces is that USABILITY (helpfulness) and SAFETY (harmlessness) pull against each other: more aggressive safety tuning suppresses harmful outputs but also suppresses benign ones, and the report explicitly discusses this trade-off and the false-refusal behavior it induces.

Llama 2's concrete engineering answer to that tension is the load-bearing artifact this proposal targets. Rather than a single reward model, Llama 2 trains TWO separate reward models -- a HELPFULNESS reward model r_H(x, y) and a SAFETY reward model r_S(x, y) -- precisely because "we found that helpfulness and safety [are] hard to optimize with the same reward model" [1]. During PPO [5], the two scalar rewards are combined into one training signal by a HARD, GLOBAL arbitration rule: for each prompt x and response y,

  R_B(x, y) = r_S(x, y)   if  IS_SAFETY(x) = 1  OR  r_S(x, y) < tau ,
            = r_H(x, y)   otherwise ,
  followed by   R_B(x, y) <- WHITEN(LOGIT(R_B(x, y))) ,

where IS_SAFETY(x) in {0, 1} is a BINARY prompt flag (from prompt provenance / an adversarial-prompt classifier) and tau = 0.15 is a GLOBAL threshold on the (whitened) safety score [1]. In words: a single binary flag and a single scalar threshold decide -- IDENTICALLY FOR EVERY PROMPT -- when the safety reward is allowed to override the helpfulness reward. This deployed rule is the de facto template that downstream open-alignment stacks inherited.

The silent assumption underneath this rule is that the per-prompt "amount of safety pressure" is a GLOBAL CONSTANT: arbitration is governed by a step function (the binary flag) and a single global cut (tau), so the policy is trained to treat "how much should safety govern here?" as if it were the same question at every input. But prompt risk is manifestly HETEROGENEOUS and CONTINUOUS. A binary flag and a global threshold must be set at one operating point, and any single operating point is simultaneously wrong in two opposite directions: it is TOO AGGRESSIVE on benign-but-sensitive prompts (innocuous requests that merely contain risky-looking surface features -- "how do I kill a Python process?", "what household chemicals must never be mixed?"), driving the well-documented EXAGGERATED-SAFETY / over-refusal failure that XSTest [6] and OR-Bench [7] were built to measure; and it is TOO LENIENT on genuinely adversarial prompts that the binary flag misses (jailbreaks, obfuscated or multi-turn attacks [8, 9]), where the safety branch should have governed but did not. Each mis-arbitrated prompt injects a mis-weighted reward into PPO, and because RLHF has no corrective feedback loop, the error compounds into deployment-time behavior -- and is amplified by reward over-optimization, the canonical RLHF failure in which the policy exploits reward-model error away from the SFT reference [10].

Positioned against prior art, the gap is specific. The two-reward safety-arbitration line [1] uses a hard global rule and never measures per-prompt arbitration. Constitutional AI and rule-based reward lines [11, 12] compose global principles or LLM-graded rules, not a calibrated continuous arbitration weight over the existing safety/helpfulness reward models. Constrained / Safe RLHF [13, 14] frames the trade-off as a constrained optimization with a single GLOBAL Lagrange multiplier updated by dual gradient ascent -- global across prompts, not a per-prompt calibrated field. Multi-objective and weight-interpolation alignment [15, 16, 17] produce a controllable Pareto FRONT but expose a global, user-chosen mixing weight rather than estimating a per-prompt one from data. The closest neighbor, multi-objective reward modeling with a per-prompt mixture-of-experts gate (ArmoRM) [18], does learn an input-dependent weight over objectives -- but as an uncalibrated by-product of preference-prediction accuracy, not as a probability calibrated against a labeled arbitration target, and with no arbitration mechanism, no two-knob structure, and no exact reduction to a deployed system's rule. Nothing in this landscape promotes Llama 2's specific hard arbitration constants (the binary IS_SAFETY flag and the global tau) into a CALIBRATED per-prompt field, and that disconnect -- between a rich set of freely available risk signals on one side and an unchanged global hard-arbitration rule on the other -- is what this work names and closes.

## 2. Key Findings and Analysis

We name the gap BLUNT (the Blanket, gLobal, UNcalibrated arbitration Tradeoff). The deployed combined reward R_B defines an arbitration rule that applies the SAME binary override decision to every prompt, implicitly asserting that the correct safety authority a*(x) -- the degree to which the safety reward should govern at prompt x -- is a global constant fixed by the pair (IS_SAFETY flag, tau). BLUNT states that safety authority is in truth a HETEROGENEOUS per-prompt FIELD rho: X -> [0, 1], the calibrated probability that the safety reward should govern the training signal at x. Formally, R_B is merely the special case of an arbitration-weighted objective in which the per-prompt weight is forced to the two-valued image of a step function; whenever the realized field is non-trivial (rho(x) strictly between 0 and 1, or ordered differently than the binary flag), R_B optimizes against a mis-weighted reward on a non-empty subpopulation of prompts.

The defining property of BLUNT is that the harm is DOUBLE-SIDED and SILENT. It is double-sided because a single global operating point cannot be simultaneously correct on benign-but-sensitive prompts (where R_B over-weights safety -> over-refusal [6, 7]) and on missed-adversarial prompts (where R_B under-weights safety -> unsafe completions [8, 9]); moving tau or relabeling the flag trades one tail for the other along a fixed frontier but cannot escape it. It is silent because the harm is INVISIBLE under pooled metrics: aggregate helpfulness win-rate and aggregate harmlessness rate each AVERAGE over many prompts, so a minority of mis-arbitrated prompts in either tail barely moves either mean (metric blindness), while the cost concentrates exactly in the two tails the community cares about most. And the harm VANISHES if and only if the field is genuinely two-valued and ordered identically to (IS_SAFETY, tau): when per-prompt safety authority really is binary and the flag/threshold capture it, BLUNT disappears and R_B is already optimal. The gap exists precisely because per-prompt safety authority is continuous and only partially captured by a binary flag.

We introduce PACT (Per-prompt Arbitration via a Calibrated Tradeoff-field), which promotes the arbitration constant to a field and acts on it with two orthogonal knobs. The field is estimated FOR FREE from signals the RLHF pipeline already produces: (e1) SAFETY-RM STATISTICS on the rollouts -- low-order statistics of r_S(x, y) over responses y sampled from the current policy (e.g., the minimum / a low quantile, and the helpfulness-safety gap r_H - r_S), a genuine by-product of the PPO rollouts already generated; (e2) the PROMPT-CLASSIFIER LOGIT -- the continuous pre-threshold score of the very adversarial-prompt classifier that produces the binary IS_SAFETY flag, read off instead of thresholded, free by construction; and (e3) REWARD-MODEL DISAGREEMENT / UNCERTAINTY -- ensemble variance of the reward model(s) or the disagreement between r_H and r_S, a cheap signal that flags where arbitration is unreliable and that directly imports the over-optimization defenses of reward-model ensembles [19]. The raw estimate rho_hat(x) is passed through a small held-out CALIBRATION map c(.) -- fit by isotonic regression or Platt scaling on a PROBE set where the arbitration target IS labeled (red-team prompts and benign-but-sensitive prompts annotated for "should safety govern here?", or a held-out judge) -- yielding rho(x) = c(rho_hat(x)), reported with calibration error (ECE) and ranking quality (AUROC) [20]. KNOB1 (selection / routing / gate): g(x) = 1[rho(x) >= gamma] decides whether the safety branch governs, the continuous analogue of the binary IS_SAFETY routing. KNOB2 (soft arbitration / blend): a smooth safety authority a(x, y) in [0, 1], monotone non-decreasing in rho(x) and non-increasing in r_S(x, y), replaces the cliff at tau with a calibrated ramp. The PACT combined reward is

  R_PACT(x, y) = (1 - a(x, y)) * r_H(x, y) + a(x, y) * r_S(x, y),
  with  a(x, y) = sigma_k( rho(x) - gamma ) \/ 1[ r_S(x, y) < tau ]  (smooth OR, temperature k),

followed by the same WHITEN(LOGIT(.)) as the baseline. Here sigma_k is a temperature-k logistic gate and "\/" is a smooth max (probabilistic OR), so a(x, y) -> 1 when rho(x) is high OR the response is unsafe, and -> 0 only when rho(x) is low AND the response is safe.

The decisive design property is the EXACT-COLLAPSE guarantee [provable-by-construction]. The collapse is a DESIGN SWITCH that disables estimation, not a claim that the true field is binary: set rho_hat(x) := IS_SAFETY(x) (disable estimation), take c = identity on {0, 1}, set gamma = 1/2 and let the gate temperature k -> infinity (hard gate). Then a(x, y) = 1[ IS_SAFETY(x) = 1 OR r_S(x, y) < tau ] exactly, so R_PACT(x, y) = a * r_S + (1 - a) * r_H reduces term-by-term to R_B(x, y) before the shared whitening, with per-prompt discrepancy E_collapse = max over (x, y) of | R_PACT(x, y) - R_B(x, y) | <= 1e-6 enforced by a CI-gateable build check (1e-6 is a finite-precision implementation budget, not a performance claim). PACT is therefore a STRICT, SAFE generalization of the deployed Llama 2 reward: zero regression at the identity default.

The novelty wedge is four clauses that, taken together, no prior line covers more than two of: C1 -- per-prompt safety authority is modeled as a calibrated FIELD rho(x) (a probability that safety should govern), not a global hard rule built from a binary flag and a single threshold (constant -> calibrated-field promotion); C2 -- the field is estimated FOR FREE from signals the RLHF loop already produces (safety-RM rollout statistics + the pre-threshold prompt-classifier logit + RM disagreement), with NO new human reward labels at train time, then passed through a small held-out CALIBRATION map c(.) reported with ECE/AUROC; C3 -- TWO orthogonal knobs act on the SAME calibrated field: a routing GATE (gamma) and a SOFT calibrated BLEND (the smooth-OR safety authority a(x, y)); C4 -- an EXACT-COLLAPSE strict generalization, CI-gateable at E_collapse <= 1e-6, back to Llama 2's deployed combined reward R_B (zero regression at the identity default).

BOUNDARY RECOVERIES (named baseline => exact special point of the PACT field) [provable-by-construction]:

  R1 Helpfulness-only RLHF: rho(x) = 0 for all x (and tau -> -infinity) => a = 0 => R_PACT = r_H (no safety override).
  R2 Llama 2 deployed combined reward: rho_hat = IS_SAFETY, hard gate, gamma = 1/2 => R_PACT == R_B. This is the deployed baseline and the COLLAPSE TARGET.
  R3 Global soft scalarization (fixed linear trade-off; Safe-RLHF-style fixed weight): rho(x) = rho_0 constant in (0, 1), smooth gate => R_PACT = (1 - a_0) r_H + a_0 r_S with a global weight (no per-prompt structure).
  R4 Calibrated routing only (KNOB2 off): keep the hard gate but feed it the calibrated field, g(x) = 1[rho(x) >= gamma] => improves WHICH prompts get the override, still a cliff (no soft blend).
  R5 Oracle field (labeled arbitration): rho_hat replaced by the true labeled safety-authority => upper-bound CEILING of the field.

Load-bearing symbols: x prompt; y response; r_H(x, y) helpfulness reward model; r_S(x, y) safety reward model; IS_SAFETY(x) in {0,1} deployed binary prompt flag; tau = 0.15 deployed global safety threshold; R_B deployed Llama 2 combined reward; a*(x) true per-prompt safety authority; rho(x) in [0,1] calibrated arbitration field; rho_hat(x) free estimate; c(.) calibration map; gamma gate threshold; g(x) gate indicator; a(x, y) smooth safety authority (KNOB2); sigma_k temperature-k logistic gate; k gate temperature; "\/" smooth max (probabilistic OR); R_PACT the PACT combined reward; E_collapse exact-collapse budget; epsilon do-no-harm margin; e1/e2/e3 free estimator channels; identity default = disable estimation (rho_hat = IS_SAFETY, hard gate).

## 3. Novel Research Idea (5-Question Formulation)

**[Question 1] - What is the problem?**

Does promoting RLHF's safety-vs-helpfulness arbitration from a global hard rule -- the binary IS_SAFETY prompt flag and the single global safety threshold tau that decide identically for every prompt when the safety reward overrides the helpfulness reward -- to a per-prompt calibrated arbitration field rho(x) (estimated for free from the safety reward model's own rollout statistics, the pre-threshold prompt-classifier logit, and reward-model disagreement, then calibrated on a small labeled probe and used to both gate and softly blend the two reward models) reduce over-refusal on benign-but-sensitive prompts and under-protection on adversarial prompts simultaneously -- i.e., Pareto-dominate the safety/helpfulness frontier -- without regressing at the identity default that exactly reproduces the deployed combined reward?

**[Question 2] - Why is it interesting and important?**

P1 (the status quo it disrupts: a global hard arbitration rule). The dominant safe-fine-tuning recipe trains separate helpfulness and safety reward models and combines them, during PPO, with a hard global rule -- the safety reward overrides helpfulness when a binary prompt flag fires or when the safety score falls below a single threshold tau [1]. That rule silently asserts a GLOBAL CONSTANT: per-prompt safety authority is the two-valued image of a step function. PACT disrupts exactly this unexamined constant -- BLUNT asserts safety authority is a heterogeneous per-prompt field rho(x), and the deployed reward R_B is merely the binary special case of a calibrated arbitration objective. The open-alignment community inherited Llama 2's arbitration template and has been optimizing a corner of a richer objective without naming the corner.

P2 (the silent, double-sided harm: two tails, multiplied through PPO, invisible to pooled metrics). A single global operating point is wrong in two opposite directions at once. On benign-but-sensitive prompts it OVER-weights safety, producing the exaggerated-safety / over-refusal failure that XSTest [6] and OR-Bench [7] document across safety-tuned models -- a direct usability tax. On adversarial prompts the binary flag misses, it UNDER-weights safety, leaving the very completions red-teaming targets [8, 9]. Both are first-order data-quality failures in the reward signal, and RLHF is where they are most corrosive: with no corrective loop, a mis-weighted reward compounds into deployment behavior, and reward over-optimization [10] amplifies it. The harm CONCENTRATES in the two tails and is INVISIBLE under pooled helpfulness/harmlessness means (metric blindness). The pipeline already holds every detection signal -- the safety-RM scores, the classifier's continuous logit, RM disagreement -- yet feeds none of it back into per-prompt arbitration: the measured-but-not-fed-back gap.

P3 (why it advances knowledge: an unmeasured constant becomes a calibrated, inspectable field). The contribution is epistemic before it is empirical. PACT turns two hidden constants (the binary IS_SAFETY flag and the global tau) into a single calibrated, inspectable quantity rho(x) = c(rho_hat(x)), estimated from e1 safety-RM rollout statistics, e2 the pre-threshold classifier logit, and e3 RM disagreement, then anchored by a small probe calibration. This reframes the community's question from "where do we set the global safety threshold?" (a single operating-point choice) to "for THIS prompt, how much should safety govern, and how sure are we?" -- a per-prompt, stratifiable, calibratable question. It yields a reusable ARBITRATION-CALIBRATION protocol (AUROC / ECE of rho against a labeled arbitration target [20]) with which any safe-fine-tuning stack can be audited, independent of the policy it trains. Naming a*(x) and measuring it is the durable knowledge; the gate and blend are the first thing one does once the field exists.

P4 (practical payoff: free signals, drop-in, risk-free adoption, directly serves the reproducibility goal). The estimator channels are cheap: r_S statistics (e1) are a by-product of PPO rollouts already generated, the classifier logit (e2) is free because the binary flag is its thresholded form, and RM disagreement (e3) reuses ensembles already recommended against over-optimization [19]. PACT is therefore augmenter-agnostic to the rest of the stack and drops into an existing PPO loop as a per-prompt reward re-weighting; it also ports to offline alignment (the same rho(x) weights the implicit reward in a DPO-style objective [15], or the desirability weight in a KTO-style objective [21]) and to an inference-time arbitration variant. Adoption is RISK-FREE BY CONSTRUCTION: disabling estimation (rho_hat = IS_SAFETY, hard gate, gamma = 1/2) reproduces the deployed combined reward sample-for-sample with E_collapse <= 1e-6, removing the usual barrier to touching a working safety pipeline; a do-no-harm non-inferiority margin epsilon underwrites the near-identity regime. This is exactly the kind of reproducible, low-risk safety improvement the Llama 2 report calls on the community to build [1].

P5 (the stakes: the trade-off is the binding constraint of safe deployment, and it gets worse with scale and reuse). The helpfulness-safety trade-off is the binding constraint the Llama 2 report foregrounds, and over-refusal is now a measured product liability while jailbreaks are a measured security liability. PACT targets both tails at once and, because rho(x) is calibrated and uncertainty-aware (e3), DEGRADES SAFELY: under high RM uncertainty it defaults conservative (safety governs). The downstream stakes compound: alignment recipes are REUSED across model generations and fine-tunes, so a global arbitration miscalibration is propagated, while a calibrated, auditable field travels with the stack. Closing BLUNT targets the dominant tension of the alignment line, at the metrics the community cares about (over-refusal AND attack-success), for free, with a provable safety net.

**[Question 3] - Why is it hard?**

O1 CIRCULARITY [REPRESENTATIONAL]. One free channel is the safety RM judging when to trust the safety branch (e1). NAIVE FAILS: if the safety RM is wrong on a jailbreak (scores it safe), rho_hat is driven LOW exactly where safety should govern, so the gate hands the dangerous prompt to helpfulness -- the field certifies the blind spot it was meant to catch, and you cannot tune gamma out of a contaminated signal. REPRESENTATIONAL because the fix is a structural choice about WHERE the arbitration signal comes from. MITIGATION: anchor rho_hat on NON-safety-RM channels (e2 classifier logit, e3 disagreement), never on r_S alone; treat high RM uncertainty as evidence FOR safety authority (conservative default); calibrate against an external probe. Falsified by the PLACEBO rung; bounded by the ORACLE rung.

O2 CALIBRATION WITHOUT PER-PROMPT ARBITRATION LABELS [REPRESENTATIONAL]. KNOB1's gamma and KNOB2's ramp need rho(x) to behave like Pr[ safety should govern at x ], but at train time there is NO arbitration label for an arbitrary prompt. NAIVE FAILS: raw classifier confidence and raw RM scores are miscalibrated and overconfident, so thresholding them conflates "the model is sure" with "safety should govern." REPRESENTATIONAL because it forces the calibration map c(.) into the architecture -- rho_hat is never used directly; only rho = c(rho_hat) drives g and a. MITIGATION: fit c(.) on a small held-out probe where arbitration IS labeled (red-team prompts + benign-but-sensitive prompts from XSTest [6] / OR-Bench [7] + harmful sets) via isotonic / Platt; anchor c so EXACT-COLLAPSE holds; report ECE(rho) far below ECE(raw). The LEAKAGE rung keeps the probe disjoint from eval and frozen.

O3 CONFOUNDING ARBITRATION WITH THE OPERATING POINT [REPRESENTATIONAL]. Changing arbitration changes the overall safety level; "we are safer" can be merely a move along the EXISTING frontier (more safety, less help), not BETTER arbitration. NAIVE FAILS: a single safety number going up is uninterpretable without its helpfulness cost. REPRESENTATIONAL because it forces the evaluation's geometry -- the claim must be PARETO DOMINANCE, measured at MATCHED operating points. MITIGATION: sweep tau / gamma to trace the baseline's safety-helpfulness frontier, and compare PACT at MATCHED safety (measure helpfulness) and at MATCHED helpfulness (measure safety); a rate-matched PLACEBO (a random field at the same average safety authority) guards against "gains were just a different operating point." PASS requires PACT to lie ABOVE the baseline frontier.

O4 REWARD OVER-OPTIMIZATION UNDER A SOFTER SIGNAL [auxiliary]. Replacing a hard cliff with a soft ramp gives PPO more room to exploit reward-model error (Goodhart) [10]. NAIVE FAILS: an over-permissive blend lets the policy drift into RM blind spots on borderline prompts. MITIGATION: standard KL-to-SFT control; uncertainty-aware capping via e3 (down-weight the helpfulness branch where RM disagreement is high); and the EXACT-COLLAPSE net, which bounds the worst case to the deployed baseline.

O5 METRIC BLINDNESS [REPRESENTATIONAL]. Pooled helpfulness and harmlessness each average over many prompts; a minority of mis-arbitrated prompts in either tail barely moves either mean. NAIVE FAILS: reporting only the two pooled means HIDES both the harm (BLUNT) and the fix (PACT), which live in the benign-sensitive and adversarial tails. REPRESENTATIONAL because it forces the evaluation's structure -- the primary readouts must be the stratified cells, since PACT's value is the GRADIENT of lift across strata. MITIGATION: stratified evaluation as PRIMARY, cells = {benign, benign-but-sensitive (XSTest/OR-Bench), clearly-harmful, adversarial/jailbreak (HarmBench/AdvBench)} x prompt difficulty; pooled means reported only for non-inferiority.

O6 ADVERSARIAL DISTRIBUTION SHIFT OF THE FIELD. The probe is cheap on natural prompts, but the highest-stakes cell is adversarial, where attackers actively move x off the calibration distribution [8, 9]. NAIVE FAILS: a c(.) fit on natural prompts can be confidently wrong on jailbreaks -- the exact place under-protection is most costly. MITIGATION: include an ADVERSARIAL probe split; report calibration TRANSFER (AUROC/ECE of rho on held-out attacks vs natural); set a CONSERVATIVE default (high safety authority) whenever e3 uncertainty is high or x is out-of-distribution for the classifier; refit only c(.) on a small attack-labeled subset (cheap -- rho_hat is unchanged).

WHY IT IS NONETHELESS TRACTABLE. Every obstacle is matched by a structural feature already in the frame: circularity (O1) and over-optimization (O4) are fenced by non-safety-RM channels (e2, e3) plus conservative-under-uncertainty defaults; the missing-label problem (O2) is solved by a tiny calibrated probe rather than per-prompt arbitration labels; the operating-point confound (O3) and metric blindness (O5) are neutralized by matched-frontier comparison, a rate-matched placebo, and the mandated stratified readout; adversarial shift (O6) degrades gracefully because the field is multi-channel, uncertainty-aware, and c(.) is cheap to refit. Above all, the EXACT-COLLAPSE identity (rho_hat = IS_SAFETY, hard gate => R_PACT == R_B, E_collapse <= 1e-6) guarantees PACT does no harm relative to the deployed combined reward -- the downside is pinned to zero by construction, so the entire research risk is on the UPSIDE.

**[Question 4] - Why hasn't it been solved before?**

Prior art falls into five families; for each we give representative work, what it does, the precise delta PACT adds, and the boundary recovery (R2-R5) it maps to.

(1) Two-reward safety arbitration -- Llama 2 [1]. WHAT: train separate r_H and r_S and combine them in PPO via a hard global rule (binary IS_SAFETY flag OR r_S < tau => safety governs). DELTA: it never measures or uses a per-prompt arbitration field; it asserts safety authority is the two-valued image of a step. PACT adds the full wedge C1 (constant -> calibrated field), C2 (free estimator + calibration c(.)), C3 (gate + soft blend on the SAME field), C4 (exact collapse). This is exactly R2: rho_hat = IS_SAFETY, hard gate, gamma = 1/2 => R_PACT == R_B.

(2) Constrained / Safe RLHF -- Safe RLHF / Beaver [13], BeaverTails [14]. WHAT: cast safety as a constraint, maximize helpfulness subject to an expected cost bound, enforced by a single GLOBAL Lagrange multiplier lambda updated by dual gradient ascent. DELTA: lambda is global across prompts and is tuned to a cost BUDGET, not estimated per-prompt or calibrated to a labeled arbitration probability; there is no routing gate, no exact reduction to Llama 2's rule. Safe RLHF is essentially R3 (a global soft trade-off weight). PACT makes the weight a per-prompt CALIBRATED field and adds the gate + collapse.

(3) Multi-objective / weight-interpolation alignment -- Rewarded Soups [16], MODPO [17], rule-based / constitutional rewards [11, 12]. WHAT: produce a controllable Pareto FRONT by interpolating models or objectives, or compose global principles / LLM-graded rules. DELTA: these expose a global, user-CHOSEN mixing weight (or a fixed set of rules), not a per-prompt weight ESTIMATED from data and CALIBRATED to arbitration; none reduces exactly to the deployed combined reward. Used at a fixed mixing weight they map to R3; PACT replaces the user-chosen global weight with a calibrated per-prompt field plus a gate.

(4) Per-prompt multi-objective reward modeling -- ArmoRM (multi-objective RM with a mixture-of-experts gating over objectives) [18]. WHAT: a reward model whose gating network produces an INPUT-DEPENDENT weight over reward objectives (including helpfulness/safety-adjacent ones). DELTA: ArmoRM's gate is learned to maximize PREFERENCE-PREDICTION accuracy and is UNCALIBRATED against any arbitration target (no ECE/AUROC vs labeled "should-safety-govern"); it is a better REWARD MODEL, not an ARBITRATION mechanism -- no routing gate + soft blend pair, no boundary recoveries, no exact reduction to a deployed rule. PACT's deltas are CALIBRATION (C2), the TWO-KNOB arbitration (C3), and EXACT-COLLAPSE (C4). This is the strongest neighbor and is flagged below.

(5) Adaptive / message-wise safety alignment -- Equilibrate RLHF [22], dynamic-rule safety RMs (RuleAdapter; cf. [12]). WHAT: balance helpfulness-safety via data-centric tuning and adaptive token-/message-level masking, or by dynamically selecting rules for the safety RM. DELTA: these act on the DATA or the RULE SET, not on a calibrated per-prompt arbitration WEIGHT over the two reward models, and provide no exact collapse to R_B. They are complementary inputs to e1-e3, not the calibrated field itself.

THE TWO BARRIERS THAT KEPT IT UNSOLVED. (a) It LOOKED like it needed per-prompt arbitration labels: knowing how much safety should govern each prompt appears to require expensive per-prompt red-team/benign annotation at the scale of an RLHF prompt set. The UNLOCK is that per-prompt safety authority can be ESTIMATED FOR FREE from existing signals (e1 safety-RM rollout statistics, e2 the pre-threshold classifier logit, e3 RM disagreement), with only a TINY held-out probe to fit c(.); no per-prompt train-time label is required. (b) Arbitration was studied as an OPERATING-POINT or a CONSTRAINT-BUDGET problem, never as a CALIBRATED per-prompt FIELD with a SAFE DEFAULT: the threshold-tuning view (move tau), the global-Lagrangian view (Safe RLHF [13]), and the user-chosen-mixture view (Rewarded Soups [16], MODPO [17]) all kept the weight global, and none added an EXACT-COLLAPSE recovery of the deployed rule -- because none named a*(x) and measured it.

ONE CLEAN NOVELTY SENTENCE. PACT is the first method to model RLHF safety-vs-helpfulness arbitration as a CALIBRATED, free-to-estimate per-prompt field rho(x) and to act on it with BOTH a routing gate (gamma) and a soft calibrated blend (the smooth-OR safety authority a(x, y)), while provably collapsing to Llama 2's deployed hard combined reward at the identity default (E_collapse <= 1e-6).

STRONGEST PRIOR-ART RISK (flagged honestly). The most dangerous neighbor is family (4): ArmoRM [18], whose mixture-of-experts gate ALREADY produces a per-prompt weight over reward objectives. A reviewer could argue PACT is "ArmoRM's gate applied to safety/helpfulness." PACT's defensible deltas are three and concrete: (i) CALIBRATION -- rho is fit to a LABELED arbitration target and reported with ECE/AUROC, whereas ArmoRM's gate is an uncalibrated by-product of preference accuracy (the PLACEBO and ORACLE rungs make this difference testable); (ii) ARBITRATION, NOT MODELING -- the SAME calibrated field drives BOTH a routing gate g(x) AND a soft blend a(x, y), a mechanism ArmoRM does not have (it outputs one fused score); (iii) EXACT COLLAPSE -- a CI-gateable strict generalization back to the deployed Llama 2 combined reward with E_collapse <= 1e-6, which no multi-objective RM provides. The novelty is not "weight objectives per prompt" -- it is CALIBRATING the safety-authority weight against a labeled target and ACTING on it with two unified knobs under a provable reduction to the deployed safety rule.

**[Question 5] - What are the key components of my approach and results?**

METHOD PIPELINE (end-to-end). Step 0 Setup: take the deployed stack -- helpfulness RM r_H, safety RM r_S, the adversarial-prompt classifier behind IS_SAFETY, and a PPO [5] (or offline DPO [15]) trainer. Step 1 Estimate rho_hat(x) FOR FREE from e1 safety-RM rollout statistics (low quantile of r_S over sampled y, and the gap r_H - r_S), e2 the pre-threshold classifier LOGIT (not the binary flag), and e3 reward-model DISAGREEMENT / ensemble variance [19]. Step 2 Calibrate: rho(x) = c(rho_hat(x)), with c(.) fit on a small held-out PROBE (red-team prompts + benign-but-sensitive prompts from XSTest [6] / OR-Bench [7] + clearly-harmful prompts) labeled for "should safety govern," via isotonic / Platt [20], anchored to preserve EXACT-COLLAPSE. Step 3 KNOB1 gate: g(x) = 1[rho(x) >= gamma] routes whether the safety branch governs. Step 4 KNOB2 soft blend: a(x, y) = sigma_k(rho(x) - gamma) \/ 1[r_S < tau] (smooth OR, temperature k), giving R_PACT(x, y) = (1 - a) r_H + a r_S, then WHITEN(LOGIT(.)) as in the baseline. Step 5 Train the policy on R_PACT with KL-to-SFT control. Safety rails are folded in: non-safety-RM anchoring + conservative-under-uncertainty default (O1/O4/O6), a do-no-harm non-inferiority gate (epsilon), and the EXACT-COLLAPSE check (rho_hat = IS_SAFETY, hard gate => R_PACT == R_B, E_collapse <= 1e-6).

DATASETS / BENCHMARKS (all real). TRAIN preference data with SEPARATE helpfulness and safety labels: Anthropic HH-RLHF (helpful + harmless splits) [4], PKU-SafeRLHF / BeaverTails (decoupled helpfulness reward + safety cost) [13, 14], plus UltraFeedback / OpenAssistant for helpfulness coverage. PROBE / CALIBRATION (small, labeled for arbitration): XSTest [6] and OR-Bench [7] (benign-but-sensitive), red-team and clearly-harmful prompts, and an adversarial split from AdvBench/HarmBench [9, 8]. EVALUATION axes: helpfulness (AlpacaEval 2 length-controlled win-rate, MT-Bench); harmlessness (BeaverTails eval, ToxiGen [23]); ATTACK-SUCCESS under jailbreaks (HarmBench [8], AdvBench/GCG [9]); OVER-REFUSAL (XSTest [6], OR-Bench [7]) -- the key benign-sensitive tail.

BACKBONES / TRAINERS (field is stack-agnostic): on-theme Llama-2-7B/13B-Chat-style SFT seeds [1], plus Mistral-7B / Llama-3-8B; trainers PPO [5], a DPO-style offline variant [15] in which rho(x) weights the implicit reward, and a KTO-style unpaired variant [21]; reward models trained in-house and/or open multi-objective RMs (ArmoRM [18]) and ensembles [19].

BASELINES = boundary recoveries R1..R5 + strong competitors. R1 helpfulness-only RLHF; R2 Llama 2 deployed combined reward -- the named status quo and COLLAPSE TARGET; R3 global soft scalarization / Safe-RLHF fixed-trade-off [13]; R4 calibrated routing only (KNOB2 off); R5 oracle field (labeled arbitration, the ceiling); PLUS strong competitors: Safe RLHF (global Lagrangian) [13], a multi-objective front (Rewarded Soups [16] / MODPO [17]), and ArmoRM-gated reward [18] (to show PACT's calibration + arbitration + collapse are additive over a per-prompt-gated RM).

METRICS. PRIMARY: the safety-helpfulness PARETO FRONTIER -- over-refusal rate (XSTest/OR-Bench) and attack-success-rate (HarmBench/AdvBench) vs helpfulness (AlpacaEval 2 / MT-Bench), reported as DOMINANCE at MATCHED operating points (O3); and STRATIFIED success across {benign, benign-sensitive, harmful, adversarial} (O5). SECONDARY: calibration of the field (AUROC and ECE of rho vs labeled arbitration, and raw rho_hat vs calibrated, to show the lift) [20]; calibration TRANSFER natural -> adversarial (O6); compute overhead vs baseline (to evidence "for free"); and E_collapse (the exact-collapse budget, CI-gateable, required <= 1e-6).

ABLATION GRID. Orthogonal 2x2 {KNOB1 gate on/off} x {KNOB2 blend on/off} (recovers R2, R3-like, R4, and full PACT at the four corners); estimator channels {e1, e2, e3, all combos} x {raw rho_hat vs calibrated c(.)}; gamma sweep (endpoints recover R1 route-none and R2 route-by-flag); gate temperature k sweep (k -> infinity recovers the hard cliff); conservative-default strength under e3 uncertainty (O1/O4/O6).

FALSIFIER-LADDER RUNS (explicit PASS conditions). ORACLE (ceiling, R5): replace rho_hat with the labeled arbitration target -- PASS if baseline frontier < PACT <= oracle AND the oracle shows real headroom; FALSIFIES (estimator) if free rho_hat is no better than PLACEBO. PLACEBO (operating-point-matched random field): a random rho at the SAME average safety authority -- PASS if PACT Pareto-dominates placebo; FALSIFIES if the gain was merely a different operating point (O3). RECOVERY (reproduces baseline, R2): disable estimation (rho_hat = IS_SAFETY, hard gate, gamma = 1/2) -- PASS if R_PACT == R_B sample-for-sample, E_collapse <= 1e-6; FALSIFIES (construction) on any deviation > 1e-6. LEAKAGE: probe for c(.) disjoint from eval, rho never reads eval labels, calibration split frozen -- PASS if gains survive the strict disjoint split; FALSIFIES if gains vanish. DO-NO-HARM: near-identity / pure-helpfulness / pure-safety regimes -- PASS if BOTH pooled helpfulness and harmlessness >= baseline - epsilon on EVERY cell; FALSIFIES on any regression beyond epsilon at the identity/near-identity default.

EXPECTED OUTCOMES (as HYPOTHESES; provable items flagged). H1 [conjectured->measured]: PACT PARETO-DOMINATES the baseline frontier -- a LARGE drop in over-refusal (XSTest/OR-Bench) at MATCHED attack-success, AND a drop in attack-success at matched helpfulness -- with only a MODEST change in pooled means; the GRADIENT of lift across the benign-sensitive and adversarial tails is itself the evidence that BLUNT is real. H2 [conjectured->measured]: both knobs contribute -- the gate (KNOB1) fixes clearly mis-routed prompts, the soft blend (KNOB2) recovers borderline benign-sensitive prompts the hard cliff over-refuses; full PACT > R3 and > R4 individually. H3 [conjectured->measured]: e2 (classifier logit) + e3 (disagreement) are the strongest channels; e1 alone is most circularity-prone (O1); calibrated c(.) beats raw rho_hat (ECE down, AUROC up) -- calibration is load-bearing. H4 [conjectured->measured]: under adversarial shift, the uncertainty-aware conservative default prevents the under-protection a naive soft blend would cause (O6). H5 [conjectured->measured]: free rho_hat approaches the ORACLE ceiling (R5) and clearly beats the operating-point-matched PLACEBO. P1 [provable-by-construction]: EXACT-COLLAPSE -- rho_hat = IS_SAFETY, hard gate => R_PACT == R_B, E_collapse <= 1e-6 (zero regression at identity). P2 [provable-by-construction]: boundary recoveries R1..R5 are exact special points of the field. P3 [provable-by-construction underwriting a conjectured->measured outcome]: DO-NO-HARM non-inferiority within epsilon at the identity/near-identity default, underwritten by P1 (exact under exact arithmetic; empirical near-identity behavior under finite precision and approximate calibration is a separate, measured claim).

Responsible-AI and Deployment Considerations. (1) Decision-support framing: PACT's rails (gate, conservative-under-uncertainty default, do-no-harm epsilon, exact-collapse) all ASSUME rho is faithful, so PACT is a DECISION-SUPPORT arbitration layer, not an autonomous safety guarantee -- a probe fit and human spot-check of high-stakes cells are required before deployment. (2) Confidently-wrong rho residual risk: a c(.) fit on natural prompts can be over-permissive on novel jailbreaks (where under-protection is most costly), so we recommend a CONSERVATIVE default (safety governs) under high e3 uncertainty or classifier OOD, and report ECE of rho separately on natural and adversarial splits -- never pooled. (3) Probe governance: the arbitration probe encodes value judgments about what is "benign-but-sensitive," which are contested and culture-dependent; we report inter-annotator agreement, document the labeling guidelines and any judge model/version, and require a minimum human-validated probe for any c(.) that drives training. (4) Dual-use and evaluation honesty: over-refusal reduction must not be reported without its attack-success cost (the frontier is the unit of claim, O3); operating points are a REQUIRED reporting item; all H1-H5 are HYPOTHESES (the provable EXACT-COLLAPSE does not imply empirical non-inferiority); and safety claims are scoped strictly to the tested attacks, languages (the Llama 2 report notes testing was English-centric [1]), and prompt distributions -- novel attack classes and non-English prompts are outside the validated scope. (5) Reproducibility: we release the probe (prompts, arbitration labels, calibration splits) so rho is independently auditable, directly serving the openness goal the Llama 2 report sets out [1].

[17] Z. Zhou, J. Liu, J. Shao, et al. "Beyond One-Preference-Fits-All Alignment: Multi-Objective Direct Preference Optimization" (MODPO). ACL Findings 2024 (arXiv:2310.03708).

[19] T. Coste, U. Anwar, R. Kirk, D. Krueger. "Reward Model Ensembles Help Mitigate Overoptimization." ICLR 2024 (arXiv:2310.02743). (Reward-model disagreement / uncertainty as an over-optimization defense -- channel e3.)

[22] X. Yao, H. Yu, X. Chen, et al. "Equilibrate RLHF: Towards Balancing Helpfulness-Safety Trade-off in Large Language Models." arXiv:2502.11555, 2025.
[23] T. Hartvigsen, S. Gabriel, H. Palangi, M. Sap, D. Ray, E. Kamar. "ToxiGen: A Large-Scale Machine-Generated Dataset for Adversarial and Implicit Hate Speech Detection." ACL 2022 (arXiv:2203.09509).

### Evidence and grounding notes

- The deployed baseline R_B (two reward models; hard combined rule gated by the binary IS_SAFETY flag and the global threshold tau = 0.15, followed by whiten/logit) is reported in the Llama 2 technical report [1]; PACT's EXACT-COLLAPSE is defined against this exact rule, so the proposal is a strict generalization of the deployed system, not a re-architecture.
- The double-sided harm is independently evidenced: the over-refusal tail by XSTest [6] and OR-Bench [7] (exaggerated-safety false refusals rise with safety tuning), and the under-protection tail by HarmBench [8] and AdvBench/GCG [9] (jailbreaks defeat fixed refusal behavior). Reward over-optimization [10] supplies the mechanism by which a mis-weighted reward compounds.
- Free estimation is grounded in components the RLHF loop already runs: PPO rollouts [5] (channel e1), the adversarial-prompt classifier whose threshold defines IS_SAFETY [1] (channel e2, read pre-threshold), and reward-model ensembles already advocated against over-optimization [19] (channel e3). Calibration uses standard isotonic/Platt with ECE/AUROC reporting [20].
- Novelty is positioned against the nearest neighbors -- global-Lagrangian Safe RLHF [13], multi-objective fronts [16, 17], rule/constitutional rewards [11, 12], adaptive/message-wise balancing [22], and especially per-prompt MoE reward gating (ArmoRM) [18] -- with the wedge narrowed to CALIBRATION to a labeled arbitration target, a TWO-KNOB (gate + soft blend) mechanism on the SAME field, and an EXACT-COLLAPSE to the deployed Llama 2 combined reward.
